mapping.a_element: family of QFTs parametrized by a moduli space M with a flat quantum connection
mapping.b_element: Frobenius manifolds with a flat structure (e.g., flat metric or flat connection)

mapping.a_element: tangent space at a point in M given by the space of observables
mapping.b_element: tangent space to a Frobenius manifold given by cohomology or deformation space

mapping.a_element: Batalin-Vilkovisky (BV) path integral approach for quantization
mapping.b_element: differential Gerstenhaber-Batalin-Vilkovisky (dGBV) algebras in the Barannikov-Kontsevich construction
algebraic structures (e.g., Gerstenhaber-Batalin-Vilkovisky algebras) underlying Frobenius manifolds

mapping.a_element: quantization machine for d-algebras using (d+1)-manifold topology
mapping.b_element: construction of Frobenius manifolds from Calabi-Yau manifolds or singularity theory

brief_justification: Both papers involve families of theories (QFTs or Frobenius manifolds) parametrized by moduli spaces with flat connections, where tangent spaces correspond to observables or cohomology. The Batalin-Vilkovisky formalism in A maps to the differential Gerstenhaber-Batalin-Vilkovisky algebras in B, and both leverage algebraic structures (d-algebras or dGBV algebras) to encode quantization or deformation theory.
